desc: The mutable users dictionary is a class attribute, so all UserService instances unexpectedly share and modify the same user state.
process uses a mutable default list, causing results to accumulate across calls that omit the data argument.
main reads result even when CONFIG["retry"] is zero or negative, which raises UnboundLocalError.
The APP_ENV default is evaluated when the function is defined, so later environment changes are ignored by new UserService instances.
_load_from_file catches every exception and silently returns potentially partial data, hiding I/O and parsing failures.
The opened file is not closed if an exception occurs before f.close(), potentially leaking file descriptors.
Randomly generated names can collide, so the returned list may contain duplicates while the users dictionary contains fewer than ten entries.
process returns either a list or False, forcing callers to handle unrelated return types and making its contract error-prone.
CONFIG["retry"] only gates a single process call and never performs retries, making the configuration behavior misleading and incorrect for its stated purpose.
CONFIG["timeout"] is never used, so configuring it has no effect.
